queue.title: Priority queue
queue.empty: No items yet. Add one to begin.

editor.whySubtitle: Why is this worth deciding about?
editor.whyPlaceholder: What's the intuition? What changes if we do this?
editor.whyTooLong: Long reasoning often signals you're describing the work, not the why. Try shortening.

importance.label: Importance
importance.hint: how critical the underlying need is

satisfaction.label: Satisfaction
satisfaction.hint: how well that need is met today

lensSubtitles.value-effort: Where the return is highest per effort

valueEffort.quickWins_one: You have {{count}} quick win. <0>{{item}}</0> has the best return — start there.
valueEffort.quickWins_other: You have {{count}} quick wins. <0>{{item}}</0> has the best return — start there if you can only ship one.
valueEffort.bigBets_one: No quick wins yet. Your best lever is a big bet: <0>{{item}}</0>.
valueEffort.bigBets_other: No quick wins yet. Your best lever is a big bet: <0>{{item}}</0>.
valueEffort.none: Nothing pops as a quick win. Re-rate items or break a big one into a smaller test.

importanceSatisfaction.opportunities_one: {{count}} opportunity gap stands out: <0>{{item}}</0> — important but barely solved today.
importanceSatisfaction.opportunities_other: {{count}} opportunity gaps. <0>{{item}}</0> is the strongest — important and barely solved today.
importanceSatisfaction.overServed_one: {{count}} item is over-served — high effort, low importance. Consider trimming.
importanceSatisfaction.overServed_other: {{count}} items are over-served — high effort on low-importance needs. Consider trimming.
importanceSatisfaction.none: No clear opportunity gap. Either you're well-served, or importance ratings need a second pass.

confidenceRisk.validate_one: {{count}} item needs validation first: <0>{{item}}</0> — risky and uncertain.
confidenceRisk.validate_other: {{count}} items need validation first. Start with <0>{{item}}</0> — riskiest and least certain.
confidenceRisk.safe_one: {{count}} item is safe to ship. <0>{{item}}</0> is the strongest of those.
confidenceRisk.safe_other: {{count}} items are safe to ship. <0>{{item}}</0> is the strongest of those.
confidenceRisk.none: Nothing screams "validate first." Confidence and risk look balanced.

recommendations.opportunityGap: High importance, low satisfaction — strong opportunity gap.
recommendations.quickWin: Quick win. Schedule it this week.
recommendations.highEffortThinPayoff: High effort, thin payoff — consider dropping.
recommendations.riskyUncertain: Risky and uncertain — break into a smaller test.
recommendations.bigBet: Big bet — worth it if you can stomach the cost.
recommendations.averageSignal: Average signal — revisit when conditions change.

empty.active: No active items. Add one from the main view.
empty.in_progress: Nothing in progress yet. Items you commit to will appear here.
empty.done: Nothing finished yet. Items you complete will appear here.
empty.dropped: Nothing dropped yet. The art of saying no comes with practice.

confirm.notePlaceholder: Add a note about the outcome (optional)

references.title: References
references.placeholder: Paste URL or drag a link here

welcome.firstHint: Add 2-3 more decisions to see priorities emerge. When you have a few items, switch lenses below for different angles.

step1.heading: What are you deciding on?
step1.subhead: One real decision you're sitting with right now.
step1.placeholder: e.g. Should I rewrite onboarding flow
step1.example1: Should I take this job offer
step1.example3: Is it time to migrate the database

step2.heading: Where does this decision belong?
step2.subhead: Group decisions by project. You can add more projects later.

step3.heading: Rate it on six dimensions
step3.subhead: Don't overthink. Gut feeling is fine — you can change anything later.

toast.soft: This project is past active focus. Decisions lose sharpness when there are too many.
toast.overloaded: This project is overloaded. Consider closing or dropping items to bring focus back.

popover.title: Projects thrive at 5–15 items
popover.softBody: Beyond 15 active items, decisions get crowded and the matrix becomes noisy. Try closing or dropping a few to restore focus.
popover.overloadedBody: This project is significantly past its useful range. Strongly consider closing or dropping items — too many active decisions defeat the purpose of prioritization.
